reason: Enforcement is structurally unnecessary under the current concept system because it does not introduce a unique concept role beyond rule application already represented by duty and consequence triggering already represented by responsibility.
systemImpact: Reintroducing enforcement as a first-class concept would duplicate existing duty-side and responsibility-side structures, confuse execution/process vocabulary with concept meaning, and create avoidable overlap in the concept graph.
removalTestResult: The system retains rule application and consequence-trigger representation without a standalone enforcement concept. Duty still represents required rule-governed conduct, and responsibility still represents post-trigger answerability and consequence linkage.
